title: montanoa tomentosa subsp. xanthiifolia
intro: Montanoa tomentosa subsp. xanthiifolia, also known as the Mexican mountaintop plant or Montanoa xanthiifolia, is a flowering plant species of the family Asteraceae, native to the mountainous regions of Mexico. The plant's medicinal properties lie in its leaves, roots, and flowers, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Montanoa tomentosa subsp. xanthiifolia include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce pain and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions.
